summary: A symmetrical ceremonial or administrative building featuring a central stepped pyramid roof with brick walls, flanked by four tall brick pillars with overhanging caps. The structure has multiple horizontal tiers with alternating brick and stone/concrete bands, white fence railings around the perimeter, and small window openings. The design suggests a formal, monumental architecture style.

shape.overall_form: rectangular with central pyramid roof and four corner pillars

rebuild_hints: Build four tall brick pillars at corners, each approximately 2 blocks wide and 14+ blocks tall
Add overhanging stone/wood caps to each pillar top
Construct central rectangular body with three visible tiers separated by stonebrick bands
Create stepped pyramid roof using progressively smaller layers of wood slabs
Place white fence blocks around entire perimeter base
Add small window openings symmetrically on visible walls
Use alternating brick and stonebrick for horizontal striping effect
Ensure bilateral symmetry across both north-south and east-west axes
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and room configuration not visible
Precise block count for roof pyramid layers difficult to determine from angles
Exact material of light-colored bands (concrete vs. quartz vs. stone) uncertain
Door/entrance mechanism details not clearly visible
Whether pillars are solid or hollow internally
